title: pollichia galeobdolon
intro: Pollichia galeobdolon, also known as the golden guineafowl or wood sorrel, is a plant species native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pollichia galeobdolon include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies such as salves and ointments for skin conditions.
